chunks.text: Faculty - EECS at Berkeley Fall Faculty Retreat 2025 Our Faculty EECS faculty have won many prestigious awards including the National Medal of Science (one each in EE and CS), the National Medal of Technology & Innovation, and six ACM A.M. Turing Awa

chnology & Innovation, and six ACM A.M. Turing Awards. The UC Berkeley Distinguished Teaching Award has been given to 13 of our faculty, and 37 have been elected to the National Academy of Engineering, one of the highest numbers of elected NAE member

, one of the highest numbers of elected NAE members in any EECS department in the country. Apply for a Faculty Position Faculty Lists Home pages, research interests, class schedules, publications, and contact information. Current Faculty All Faculty

moriam We commemorate our colleagues who helped to shape this department as we celebrate their legacy. Remember Faculty We Have Lost EE Prof. Joseph Thomas Gier and his patent for the Black Body Reflectometer Reintroducing Joseph Thomas Gier Meet EE

ctometer Reintroducing Joseph Thomas Gier Meet EE Prof. Joseph Gier (1910-1961), the first tenured black professor in the U. C. system and the first tenured black faculty member in a STEM field—and the second in any field—at a top-ranked, predominant

e second in any field—at a top-ranked, predominantly white university in the country. He was also a world expert in the field of thermal and luminous radiation and was considered by many at the time to be the “best laboratory instructor ever to teach
o be the “best laboratory instructor ever to teach in electrical engineering at Berkeley.” Learn about Joseph Gier View Open Faculty Positions
